ingredients.name: Large eggs
ingredients.notes: Standard large chicken eggs, used for most egg-based recipes.

ingredients.name: Whole milk
ingredients.notes: Cow’s milk with about 3.25% fat; can be substituted with lower-fat milk in many recipes.

ingredients.name: Unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Preferred in cooking and baking so you can control the salt level.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use extra-virgin for finishing and light sautéing; common pantry staple.

ingredients.name: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Coarse-grained salt commonly used for cooking and seasoning.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black peppercorns for seasoning.

ingredients.name: Cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: Shredded sharp or mild cheddar for omelettes, scrambles, and quiches.

ingredients.name: Mozzarella cheese
ingredients.notes: Low-moisture shredded mozzarella, melts smoothly in baked egg dishes.

ingredients.name: Baby spinach
ingredients.notes: Tender spinach leaves, loosely packed, great for omelettes and quiches.

ingredients.name: Button mushrooms
ingredients.notes: White or cremini mushrooms, sliced; good for sautéeing in egg dishes.

ingredients.name: Heavy cream
ingredients.notes: High-fat dairy cream, often used in custards and rich scrambled eggs.

ingredients.name: Granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: White granulated sugar for desserts and sweet egg custards.

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Standard wheat flour suitable for general baking and pie crusts.

ingredients.name: Unbaked pie crust
ingredients.notes: Single 9-inch pie crust, homemade or store-bought, used for quiches and custard pies.

ingredients.name: Green onion
ingredients.notes: Also called scallions; used sliced as a mild onion garnish or filling.

ingredients.name: Garlic clove
ingredients.notes: Fresh garlic cloves, minced or sliced for aromatics.

ingredients.name: Vanilla extract
ingredients.notes: Pure vanilla extract used for custards and sweet bakes.

ingredients.name: Non-stick cooking spray
ingredients.notes: Aerosol cooking spray used to lightly coat pans and skillets.

ingredients.name: Bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Red, yellow, or green bell pepper, diced; adds sweetness and crunch to egg dishes.

ingredients.name: Bacon strips
ingredients.notes: Pork bacon, cooked and crumbled or served alongside egg breakfasts.

product_categories.name: Pans & Skillets
product_categories.description: Non-stick and stainless steel pans, skillets, and frying pans optimized for cooking eggs.

product_categories.name: Cooking Utensils
product_categories.description: Spatulas, whisks, ladles, and other tools for scrambling, flipping, and serving eggs.

product_categories.name: Bakeware
product_categories.description: Pie dishes, ramekins, and baking trays for quiches, custards, and frittatas.

product_categories.name: Accessories
product_categories.description: Oven mitts, trivets, and small kitchen accessories to support egg cooking.

pantry_items.name: Kosher salt
pantry_items.notes: Coarse kosher salt in a cardboard canister; almost empty.

pantry_items.name: Olive oil
pantry_items.notes: Extra-virgin olive oil bottle, about one-third full.

pantry_items.name: Large eggs
pantry_items.notes: Open carton with 8 large eggs remaining.

pantry_items.name: Granulated sugar
pantry_items.notes: Stored in an airtight canister on the counter.

pantry_items.name: All-purpose flour
pantry_items.notes: Unbleached flour in a sealed bag inside a bin.

recipe_steps.instructionText: Crack the eggs into a medium bowl. Add a pinch of salt and pepper, then whisk vigorously until the mixture looks smooth and slightly frothy.

Place a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add a small drizzle of olive oil. When the oil shimmers, add the sliced mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the spinach to the pan and cook, tossing with the mushrooms, just until the leaves are wilted and any excess moisture has evaporated. Spread the vegetables in an even layer in the skillet.
Pour the beaten eggs evenly over the mushroom and spinach mixture. Tilt the pan to distribute the eggs, gently pushing the set edges toward the center while letting uncooked egg flow to the edges.
When the top is mostly set but still slightly glossy, fold the omelette in half and cook for another 30–60 seconds to finish. Slide onto a plate and serve immediately.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt and pepper, and whisk until the yolks and whites are fully combined and slightly foamy.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat and melt the butter. Pour in the eggs and let them sit for a few seconds, then gently push the curds from the edges toward the center with a spatula.
Continue gently stirring until the eggs are just set and still slightly glossy. Remove from the heat immediately and serve warm.
Beat the eggs with salt, pepper, and a splash of milk in a small bowl until well combined and slightly bubbly.
Warm a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a little butter or olive oil. Pour in the eggs and let them cook undisturbed until the edges begin to set.
Use a spatula to gently lift the edges, letting uncooked egg flow underneath. When mostly set, fold the omelette in half and cook briefly until the center is just set, then serve.
Place the eggs in a single layer in a saucepan and cover with cold water by about 1 inch.
Set the pan over medium-high heat and bring the water to a full rolling boil, then immediately cover the pan and turn off the heat.
Let the eggs sit in the hot water for the desired doneness, then transfer them to an ice bath to cool completely before peeling.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt, and whisk until smooth. Lightly grease a microwave-safe mug or ramekin with cooking spray.
Pour the egg mixture into the mug and microwave in short bursts, stirring once or twice, until the eggs are just set and fluffy. Let cool slightly before serving to kids.
Whisk together the eggs, milk, sugar, and vanilla in a mixing bowl until the sugar is dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Divide the custard into ramekins, place them in a baking dish, and add hot water to come halfway up the sides. Bake until the centers are just set and a slight wobble remains.

reviews.comment: Super fast, fluffy scrambled eggs that really are done in under 10 minutes.
Great dairy-free omelette with plenty of mushrooms and spinach, very flavorful.
My kids loved this mug egg snack and it was so easy to throw together.
Creamy custard with just the right amount of sweetness and a nice vanilla flavor.
These boiled eggs peeled easily and came out perfectly every time I tried it.
Perfect make-ahead vegetarian breakfast, very tasty and under 200 calories.
Simple but delicious avocado toast with egg, filling without feeling heavy.
Very soft, custardy eggs with fresh herb flavor, great for a slower weekend breakfast.
Excellent value for the price; non-stick surface works well for scrambled eggs.
Great all-purpose non-stick skillet, heats evenly and is perfect for big egg breakfasts.
The hinged design makes flipping omelettes easy and cleanup is quick.
